text: There is an odd sense of stillness as I look around. The sharp, artificial light reflecting off the glass and the quiet hallways make me feel like I am caught between worlds: not quite inside nor outside, not quite present nor absent. In the dimness, the mind wanders to the nature of emptiness—how places can hold echoes of purpose even when unoccupied. I feel contemplative, tinged with a subtle unease, as if waiting for meaning to reveal itself in the quiet.
